successCriteria.description: src/modules/system directory exists with system.interface.ts, system.service.ts, system.module.ts, and index.ts files
src/modules/system/system.interface.ts exports ProcessInfo and ISystemService interfaces
src/modules/system/system.service.ts implements ISystemService with getProcessInfo method returning ProcessInfo
src/modules/system/system.module.ts defines Fastify plugin with GET /pid endpoint returning process info
tests/unit/modules/system/system.service.spec.ts exists with Vitest tests verifying SystemService.getProcessInfo returns valid pid
All TypeScript files compile without errors using TypeScript compiler
Module follows established project structure patterns with no circular dependencies

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
Modular monolith architecture: New module must follow established module structure patterns
Dependency direction: No reverse dependencies permitted from other modules to system module
TypeScript and Node.js 20: Code must be compatible with project tech stack
outOfScope: Phase 2 concerns: Full implementation of SystemService with process ID retrieval
Phase 3 concerns: Controller creation and route validation
Phase 4 concerns: RBAC enforcement and audit logging
Integration with existing employee, policy, balance, leave, or notification modules
Audit record creation for state-changing operations
Production-ready error handling beyond basic implementation
Comprehensive input validation beyond basic TypeScript types
Logging implementation beyond basic console logging

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions 'Jest unit tests' but the project uses Vitest. Should tests follow Vitest patterns as shown in the architecture-agent output?
options: Use Vitest as shown in architecture-agent output
Use Jest as originally mentioned in intent

ambiguities.description: Should the system.module.ts Fastify plugin be registered at a specific route prefix (e.g., '/system') or at root?
Should the ProcessInfo interface include additional fields beyond pid for future extensibility?
options: Include additional fields like uptime, version, etc.
